SEARCH
Module com.example.simul2d
Package com.example.simul2d.JavaFX

Class TimeController

java.lang.Object
com.example.simul2d.JavaFX.TimeController
All Implemented Interfaces:
NeedsSimulationState
public class TimeController extends Object implements NeedsSimulationState
Controller for the time control panel of the simulation UI.

Provides buttons and displays for:

  • Play / pause toggle
  • Speed increase / decrease
  • Direct speed setting (1×, 2×, 3×)
  • Manual step execution with a configurable number of steps

This controller implements NeedsSimulationState so it can be injected with the shared SimulationState and refresh its UI accordingly.

    Sets the simulation speed to 2×.

  • Constructor Details

    • TimeController

      public TimeController()

  • Method Details

    • handlePlayPause

      public void handlePlayPause()
      Handles the play/pause button action by posting a PauseCommand to the simulation input queue and updating the button text to "Play". (The actual play/pause state will be reflected on the next refreshUI() call.)

    • handleSpeedUp

      public void handleSpeedUp()
      Handles the "speed up" button by posting an IncreaseSpeedCommand.

    • handleSpeedDown

      public void handleSpeedDown()
      Handles the "speed down" button by posting a DecreaseSpeedCommand.

    • handleSpeed1

      public void handleSpeed1()
      Sets the simulation speed to 1×.

    • handleSpeed2

      public void handleSpeed2()
      Sets the simulation speed to 2×.

    • handleSpeed3

      public void handleSpeed3()
      Sets the simulation speed to 3×.

    • handleStep

      public void handleStep()
      Handles the manual step button.

      Reads the count from countField, validates that it is a positive integer, and then posts a StepCommand with that number of steps to the simulation input queue. If the input is invalid, an error message is shown in statusLabel.

    • setSimulationState

      public void setSimulationState(SimulationState state)
      Injects the shared SimulationState.

      This method should be called right after FXMLLoader.load() from the application thread. It stores the reference and immediately updates the time label if the node is already constructed.

      Specified by:
      setSimulationState in interface NeedsSimulationState
      Parameters:
      state - the simulation state, may be null

    • refreshUI

      public void refreshUI()
      Refreshes all UI elements that depend on the simulation state.

      Updates the time and speed labels with the current values, and sets the play/pause button text accordingly. This method is typically called from a periodic timer or after each simulation step.

      Specified by:
      refreshUI in interface NeedsSimulationState
